Patch Set 1:

That's very odd...  Particularly odd, given that this is the ofd lvb code, which isn't even used on the MDT.  (Of course you might be using a single node, but all your hung threads are MDT threads.)

If it's really causing the hang, you've probably got other stuck threads somewhere else, Oleg...?  Perhaps finding them would make things clearer...

Patch Set 3:

Patrick

So, in Cray's testing, you get exactly the same kind of crash as reported in LU-11629 even using the current version of this patch?

Peter

Patch Set 3:

Peter,

The crash in LU-11629 is similar, but it's in a different function modified by the original patch (ie the patch that introduced the bug being fixed here).

Specifically, it's in the MDT - not OFD - version of this function.  So while it's likely a similar issue, it's not *exactly* the same.  Since Oleg declared LU-11629 to be a duplicate of this, I assume he intends for the patch here to fix that as well.  (Unless I'm *really* confused, there's no way this exact patch could fix the crashin LU-11629.)

(Also, when I reported it on the original Gerrit (https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/32832/), Alex replied saying "I'm updating the patch".  I assume this is what he was referring to.)

If I'm wrong or we're going to make another patch, I'm happy to switch my -1 to a +1 again - The patch is not wrong, it just doesn't fix another (closely related, or so Oleg and I believe) issue.

ok Patrick. So if you don't agree with Oleg's analysis that LU-11629 is a duplicate of this issue that sounds like we should reopen LU-11629 until we are able to test to prove/disprove this theory but I don't see why that should stop what is expected to be the fix to LU-11483 landing. Could you please remove your -1? Thanks!

Patch Set 3:

As I noted in LU-11629:

Essentially, exactly the same problem exists in two places in the code.  One crashes with the signature given in LU-11483, one is the signature reported here in LU-11629.  It's simply that the same fix has to be applied in two places.  I believe that's what Oleg was indicating by marking this as a duplicate of LU-11483.

I was just noting that in that case, the patch has to add that second case.  Based on what Alex said in https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/32832/, I think he's doing that.

But I'm happy to remove my -1.

commit.subject: LU-11483 ldlm ofd_lvbo_init() and mdt_lvbo_fill() create env
commit.message:
LU-11483 ldlm ofd_lvbo_init() and mdt_lvbo_fill() create env

on demand as in very few cases it's called with env undefined.
it would be a very big patch to pass env through all the path
chains..
